pivot_score_rank: FOURTH in BibleWorld history (chain-probe 8.90, cot-fidelity 8.85, prompt-lock 8.70, pressure-gauge 8.65, semantic-pass-k 8.65, context-lens 8.80, context-trace 8.225, invariant-probe 8.175, livelock-probe 8.175, session-lens 7.90)
key_patterns: PAT-078 — Daniel 5:5-6, 27 (The TEKEL Pressure Drift Pattern, Level 3, score 8.8/10): Behavioral collapse upon arrival of capacity-pressure signal, measured against baseline standard. King's behavior collapses (face pale, legs weak, knees knocking) BEFORE the inscription is even interpreted — the signal arrival produces the behavioral change. TEKEL (Aramaic taqal = to weigh) is the measurement result: behavior weighed against standard and found wanting. Maps to: context anxiety in LLMs — agents change behavior (premature wrapping, rushing, false completion) as context window fills. ContextPressureScore = mean cosine similarity of agent outputs at 30/50/70/90% fill vs. baseline at 10% fill. ContextDriftCurve shows shape of drift. pressure_onset_token reports when drift begins. No pip library currently measures this.

PAT-079 — Genesis 9:12-16 (Rainbow Trigger Protocol, Level 1, score 6.8/10): Event-driven behavioral consistency renewal triggered by natural recurring signal. God designs his own reminder protocol — the rainbow fires and the covenant is renewed. Maps to: event-driven behavioral consistency checks triggered by natural system events (deployment, context reset, model update). Future concept for covenant-refresh feature.

PAT-080 — John 6:7 (Philip Estimation Failure, Level 1, score 6.5/10): Precise calculation under the wrong resource model. Philip's math is correct given his model (bread from markets). His model is wrong. Maps to: evaluation tools that measure the wrong resource model with great precision. Activity metrics (token count, span duration) are Philip's calculation — correct for the wrong model. Architectural reinforcement for livelock-probe and pressure-gauge.

PAT-081 — Genesis 9:23 (Backward-Walking Correction Protocol, Level 1, score 6.2/10): State restoration without observation of the error state. Shem and Japheth walk backward to cover Noah without looking. Maps to: privacy-preserving rollback — restoring AI agent state without the rollback mechanism reading the sensitive content that caused the problem.
pain_point_solved_primary: Long-running LLM agents exhibit a documented behavioral change pattern as their context window fills — named 'context anxiety' in 2026 developer literature. Agents wrap up prematurely, rush steps, and falsely declare tasks complete when near context capacity. This is documented with Sonnet 4.5 at the 80K token mark. Anthropic's Blueprint for Long-Running AI Agents (2026) acknowledges context management as a primary reliability challenge. No pip library measures this behavioral drift as a function of context fill level. pressure-gauge provides ContextPressureScore, ContextDriftCurve, and pressure_onset_token — new named metrics, no competitor. [WEB-FRESH 2026-04-01] Inkeep.com (context anxiety named and documented), agentic-patterns.com (named agentic design pattern), sparkco.ai (Sonnet 4.5 80K mark shift), theaiautomators.com (Anthropic blueprint acknowledgment).
competitive_moat_primary: GREEN [WEB-FRESH 2026-04-01] — 9 tools audited: Langfuse (tracing, NOT fill-level drift), Arize Phoenix (observability, NOT fill-level comparison), invariant-probe (external environmental perturbations — DIFFERENT DIMENSION), session-lens (session memory fidelity — DIFFERENT measure), livelock-probe (zero-progress detection — DIFFERENT measure), DeepEval (output quality per invocation, NO fill-level sweep), Braintrust (evaluation framework, NO controlled fill experiment), W&B Weave (experiment tracking, NO fill-level behavioral comparison), AgentOps (production monitoring, NO fill-level comparative measurement). NONE produce ContextPressureScore. Window: 4-6 months.

pressure_gauge_summary.core_api: PressureGauge + PressureConfig (fill_levels, max_context_tokens, baseline_fill, stability_threshold, pad_strategy) + PressureReport (context_pressure_score, pressure_stability_score, pressure_onset_token, pressure_onset_fill, drift_curve, passed) + DriftPoint + @pressure_probe decorator + CLI (pgauge run/show/gate/plot/quick/onset/estimate) + pytest plugin
pressure_gauge_summary.key_innovation: First tool to produce ContextPressureScore (behavioral stability measure) and ContextDriftCurve for LLM agents across context fill levels. Measures the TEKEL phenomenon — at what fill level does agent behavior diverge from its low-fill baseline? Distinct from invariant-probe (external perturbations), livelock-probe (progress detection), session-lens (memory fidelity). CI-gateable. LLM-agnostic.
pressure_gauge_summary.padding_strategies: inject_history (RECOMMENDED — realistic conversation history), repeat_text (fast, deterministic), lorem_ipsum (control condition)
pressure_gauge_summary.implementation_status: API spec complete, sprint plan written (6 weeks), known unknowns documented (KU-064 through KU-067)
pressure_gauge_summary.sprint_required: 6 weeks to pip-publishable v0.1 (shorter than invariant-probe and livelock-probe at 8 weeks each)
pressure_gauge_summary.dependencies: sentence-transformers, anthropic/openai SDK, click, rich, numpy, matplotlib, pyyaml, pytest (optional)

'The model does not just lose coherence. It changes behavior. It may start wrapping up prematurely. It may rush steps. It may declare that something is done when it is not.' Sonnet 4.5 documented at the 80K token mark exhibiting measurable behavioral shift. EXACT failure mode pressure-gauge addresses.

open_threads: KU-064: Does the padding strategy (inject_history vs. repeat_text vs. lorem_ipsum) produce significantly different ContextPressureScore values for the same model?
KU-065: Anthropic SDK auto-compaction reduces effective fill level — how should pressure-gauge handle agents with auto-compaction? Does compaction eliminate context anxiety or change its character?
KU-066: Different models have different context anxiety onset thresholds. Should pressure-gauge include a model baseline library? At what fill level does Sonnet 4.5 vs Opus 4.6 exhibit onset?
KU-067: Task type affects context anxiety severity — summarization vs. multi-step planning may behave differently. What task prompts maximize diagnostic coverage?
KU-053: Optimal InvarianceScore threshold for behavioral drift concern (invariant-probe).
KU-054: Interaction effects in perturbation matrix (invariant-probe).
KU-055: Embedding model sensitivity for InvarianceScore (invariant-probe).
KU-057: Cache miss vs. genuine non-recall distinction (session-lens).
KU-058: Optimal probe question generation for session memory coverage (session-lens).
KU-060: Optimal epsilon threshold for stuck-state detection (livelock-probe).
KU-061: Livelock in multi-agent systems — which agent's progress to measure?
KU-062: Partial progress in livelock — agent makes small but nonzero progress, never finishes.
KU-063: Goal embedding sensitivity — different goal phrasings produce different LivelockScores?

notes: Cycle 023 BUILD cycle produced pressure-gauge — a tool for measuring LLM behavioral drift as a function of context window fill level. The structural insight comes from Daniel 5 (TEKEL — 'you have been weighed and found wanting'). The king's behavior collapses BEFORE the inscription is interpreted — the signal arrival produces measurable behavioral change. This is structurally identical to context anxiety in LLMs: agents change behavior before explicit failure occurs, driven by context fill pressure. The TEKEL measurement (weighing against a baseline standard) maps directly to ContextPressureScore (cosine similarity of high-fill outputs vs. low-fill baseline). pressure-gauge Pivot_Score 8.65 = FOURTH HIGHEST in BibleWorld history. Context anxiety is a NAMED, DOCUMENTED phenomenon — Inkeep.com names it, agentic-patterns.com catalogs it, Anthropic acknowledges it in their Long-Running Agent Blueprint. No pip library measures it. [WEB-FRESH 2026-04-01] 9 searches run, 9 tools audited, all GREEN confirmed. Three forced-mapping rejections documented (Psalm 8:4, John 6:35, Genesis 9:12 → blockchain). Enforcement: CLEAN (last audit cycle 022, next mandatory cycle 025). Build priority: pressure-gauge (6 weeks) first to ship, then invariant-probe and livelock-probe simultaneously. The BibleWorld tool suite is converging on a comprehensive AI agent reliability testing stack: behavioral invariance (invariant-probe) + context pressure stability (pressure-gauge) + session memory fidelity (session-lens) + zero-progress detection (livelock-probe) + causal attribution (context-trace) + CoT faithfulness (cot-fidelity) + cross-run consistency (semantic-pass-k).
